calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-04-2021 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George Sullivan appeared by telephone for and with Albert Boro by zoom and for Leal Buck.

The Court asked the parties if they care to add anything on the storage fees, and questions why they didn't just distribute the personal property and not incur a few thousand dollars in storage fees. Mr. Sullivan stated that his Client wasn't appointed until October 29, 2019, that his Client cleaned out the Decedent's house, that the statutory time to file a claim expired on October 29, 2020 and that his Client did not feel comfortable distributing to himself without a Court order. Mr. Boro stated that the Decedent saved everything, that they staged the house to sell, that there was clothing and household furnishings, and that when the Decedent passed away, they moved everything to storage. Mr. Boro also stated that his intention is to do a pass to see if there is any memorabilia or photos for the family and himself, that the rest of the items will be disposed of and that there really is nothing that he sees of value. Mr. Sullivan stated that the records that were provided for the extraordinary fees are contemporaneous records. The First and Final Account and Report, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-29-2019 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-09-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Of Will Contest
Off Calendar Sep-24-2019 Continued To Oct-29-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: George Sullivan for Leal Buck and Albert Boro (proposed Co-Executors).

The Court questions whether Ms. Madjus as the proposed Custodian of the CUTMA account had authority to compromise this claim on behalf of David Competente (Minor), or whether a Guardian Ad Litem is needed for him. The Court also noted that it is not inclined and does not think that the $20,000.00 could be distributed, that it would need to be a preliminary distribution and that it requires a petition. Mr. Sullivan stated that Mr. Boessenecker has offered to stipulate to the $20,000.00 being paid at final distribution, and to Leal Buck and Albert Boro be appointed as the Co-Executors on October 9th. Mr. Sullivan also asked if he could do an ex parte petition to appoint a Guardian Ad Litem and stated that he thought the Custodian could sign off on the settlement agreement. The Court noted that Ms. Madjus is only responsible for the funds that are in the CUTMA account, but not for any other anticipated assets. The Court also questions whether an amendment is needed for the change in distribution of the $20,000.00 and stated that Counsel needs to determine how to appropriately proceed. The Petition for Approval of Settlement of Will Contest is continued to October 29, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
